assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend has given rise to a recognised receivable/payable and therefore potentially creates a foreign currency exposure within the group.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to changes in fair value of a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of a recognised asset or liability or a highly probable forecast transaction attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model relevant to foreign currency exposures in consolidation.
